visual_elements.description: Entropy is a measure of the disorder or randomness within a system.
In any energy exchange, if no energy enters or leaves the system, the potential for entropy increases, often leading to increased chaos and heat loss over time due to irreversible processes.

The second law of thermodynamics states that in an isolated system, the total entropy can only remain constant or increase; it cannot decrease naturally without external influence (entropy reduction).
Entropy is related to energy dispersal and efficiency within a process – for instance, engines have maximum possible work output at zero entropy change but real systems always generate some entropy.
